title: Setup Database & Alembic Migrations
description: Prepare the PostgreSQL database using Docker and set up Alembic to manage schema changes for the FastAPI application.
ai_instructions: You are a database administrator. Ensure the setup is clean and migrations are repeatable using Python tools.
execution_steps: Create a 'docker-compose.yml' file at the project root to define the PostgreSQL service, using environment variables for credentials.
Install Alembic, SQLAlchemy, and psycopg2-binary into the backend virtual environment.
Run 'alembic init backend/migrations' to create the migration environment.
Update 'backend/alembic.ini' with the correct database URL, sourcing from environment variables.
Modify 'backend/migrations/env.py' to ensure it can see the database models.
Create a new migration revision using 'alembic revision -m "Create initial tables"'.
Copy the SQL from DATA-001, DATA-002, DATA-003, and DATA-004 into the upgrade function of the new migration file.
Add `@trace TASK-006` to the new migration file.
definition_of_done: 'docker-compose up -d' starts the PostgreSQL container successfully.
'alembic upgrade head' runs successfully from within the backend directory, creating all tables.
The backend application can establish a connection to the database.
